Enron's Dabhol Pwr To Be Sold To Indian State, State Power Corp. Says
2001-10-23 16:00 (New York)


     (Adds Enron's comment in sixth paragraph.)

     New Delhi, Oct. 23 (Bloomberg) -- Enron Corp.'s Indian unit will be sold to the state power company, the Maharashtra State Electricity Board said in a statement.

     The sale is part of a $1.5 billion credit line Enron Corp. extended to the state power company, the statement said.

     The sale is expected to close in the third quarter, the statement said.

     Enron shares fell $1.50 to $17.50 in New York Stock Exchange trading.

     The sale of Enron's stake in the Dabhol power project in western India is the first step in a $2.9 billion rescue plan for the project, which is the largest foreign investment in India.

The company's shares have fallen by more than half since Oct. 16, when the Securities and Exchange Commission began investigating the company's dealings.

Enron's Dabhol Pwr To Be Sold To Indian State, State Power Corp. Says
2001-10-23 16:00 (New York)

     (Adds details of DPC's offer, background, byline.)

     New Delhi, Oct. 23 (Bloomberg) -- Enron Corp.'s $2.9 billion
power purchase agreement with the Maharashtra State Electricity
Board will be sold to the Indian state, the state power company said
in a statement.

     The Maharashtra State Electricity Board, which is in the process of
buying the Dabhol power plant, said it has received an offer from a
partner for the project, Enron Corp.'s Indian unit, Enron Power Development
Co., the statement said.

     The Maharashtra State Electricity Board said it will consider the offer
in consultation with the state government, the statement said.

customer service department.  We are looking for people who are
